assumptions: The intra-group dividend has been declared and a foreign-currency-denominated intra-group receivable has been recognised.
The question concerns hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements under IFRS 9.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: A recognised foreign-currency monetary receivable is a candidate hedged item within the IFRS 9 hedge accounting model, subject to the specific consolidated-group rules for intragroup items.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: Cash flow hedge accounting is a distinct top-level IFRS hedge accounting model and is part of the peer set for assessing foreign-currency risk hedging treatments.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: Net investment hedging is a separate top-level IFRS hedge accounting model for foreign exchange risk and is specifically addressed by IFRIC 16.
